findings.title: Public administrative port exposed
findings.description: SSH is open to the internet on a production-facing security group.
findings.impact: Attackers can attempt brute-force, credential stuffing, or exploit management-plane services.
findings.remediation: Restrict administrative access to a VPN, bastion host, or approved office ranges.

findings.title: Pipeline token has broad write permissions
findings.description: The workflow grants write permissions broadly instead of defining least-privilege permissions per job.
findings.impact: A compromised workflow step can modify code, releases, or deployment artifacts.
findings.remediation: Set permissions at workflow and job level and grant only the scopes required by each job.

findings.title: Container runs without a non-root user
findings.description: The Dockerfile does not define a non-root runtime user.
findings.impact: If the container is compromised, the attacker may gain elevated privileges inside the container.
findings.remediation: Create a dedicated user and switch to it before runtime commands.

findings.title: Missing readiness probe
findings.description: The workload has no readiness probe.
findings.impact: Traffic can be routed to pods before they are ready to serve requests.
findings.remediation: Add a readinessProbe that checks a lightweight application health endpoint.
